translations.verbatim_quote: The demigods, being pleased by sacrifices, will also please you, and thus, by cooperation between men and demigods, prosperity will reign for all.
The demigods, being pleased by sacrifices, will also please you, and thus, by cooperation between men and demigods, prosperity will reign for all.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 1632 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The demigods are empowered administrators of material affairs. The supply of air, light, water and all other benedictions for maintaining the body and soul of every living entity is entrusted to the demigods, who are innumerable assistants in different parts of the body of the Supreme Personality of…

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.11 ‘Bhavayata, you nourish; devan, the gods, Indra and others; anena, with this sarifice. Let te devah, those gods; bhavayantu, nourish; vah, you-make you contented with rainfall etc.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-3-11-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: The Advaita reading: the mutual nourishment is cosmological-natural: oblations to the gods reach the celestial agents (Indra et al.) responsible for natural processes (especially rain), and they reciprocate by sustaining the world. The wheel is mutual; withdrawal from one side breaks the other. The verse uses parasparam (mutually) to insist on the bidirectionality.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.11 ‘By this,’ i.e., by this sacrifice, you propitiate the gods who form My body and have Me as their Self. For Sri Krsna will say later on: ‘For I am the only enjoyer and the only Lord of Sacrifices’ (9.24).
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-3-11-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: The Vishishtadvaita reading: the devas are real persons with the Supreme Person as their inner Self. The mutual nourishment is real cosmic exchange, not metaphor. parasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha — by mutual nourishment you attain the supreme good. Engineering layers must NOT collapse devas into 'team abstractions' — the Vishishtadvaita reading specifically rules this out.

positions.rendering: demigods, being pleased by sacrifices, will also please you
celestial gods will be pleased; cooperation between humans and the celestial gods
the gods are pleased by oblations and reciprocate with rain, food, and worldly goods
the devas are real persons with the Supreme Person as their inner Self; mutual nourishment is real cosmic exchange

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: The four readings agree on the mechanism (mutual nourishment) but differ on the ontology of the devas. Prabhupada and Mukundananda use 'demigods/celestial gods' with personal reference; the Advaita reading takes them as cosmological agents responsible for natural processes (rain especially); the Vishishtadvaita reading insists they are real persons with the Supreme Person as their inner Self. Engineering layers should NOT collapse devas into 'team abstractions' — the Vishishtadvaita reading specifically rules this out. The analog must be acknowledged as analog.

literal_meaning: By this (yajña) nourish the gods; let those gods nourish you; nourishing each other, you shall attain the supreme good.
traditional_meaning_consensus: 3.11 names the mutual nourishment. devān bhāvayatānena — by this (yajña) nourish the gods; te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ — let the gods nourish you. parasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha — nourishing each other, you shall attain the supreme good. Per the Advaita reading: the gods are pleased by oblations and reciprocate with rain, food, and worldly goods. Per the Vishishtadvaita reading: the devas are real persons; the Supreme Person abides as their inner Self; the mutual nourishment is real cosmic exchange. The verse states: the wheel is mutual; withdrawal from one side breaks the other.
